item.title: The U.S. last beat screwworm in 1966. Can current leaders learn from the past’s playbook?
item.description: Faced with a modern outbreak that could endure for decades, historical documents are offering officials solutions to eradicate the parasite again.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: USDA defends screwworm response amid congressional fingerpointing
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Mexican man dies in ICE custody in Laredo, at least the 20th fatality this year
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Less diverse history, more Bible stories in public schools get initial OK by Texas board
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost one year after deadly flood
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: Where is screwworm in Texas? Track cases here.
item.dc:creator: Berenice Garcia

item.title: Cornyn and Crockett remain top skeptics of their party’s U.S. Senate nominees after primary losses
item.description: The outgoing lawmakers have cast doubt on whether their respective primary opponents, Ken Paxton and James Talarico, can win in November.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas lawmakers want fixes to statewide voter registration system ahead of midterms
item.description: The system has been riddled with problems since it was updated last year. Some lawmakers and election officials want it fixed by November.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Contreras

item.title: Congressional panel demands Travis County DA’s records on immigration prosecution
item.description: Three GOP judiciary committee members, including Texas Rep. Chip Roy, accused District Attorney José Garza of maintaining policies that “undermined the rule of law.”
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: Texas leaders are asking data centers how much water they use. Most aren’t responding.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Data centers not welcome: New poll shows most Texans oppose them in their communities

item.title: Ken Paxton and James Talarico are neck and neck in U.S. Senate race, new poll finds

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court denies Texas death row inmate’s intellectual disability claim
